libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Configure the generated test JVM with `--add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED` before exercising OGNL internals that inspect or invoke JDK/private members. Keep OGNL security-manager scenarios self-contained in test code: set and restore `ognl.security.manager` around tests that intentionally cover OGNL's sandbox/force-disable behavior, and tolerate `System.setSecurityManager` being unsupported on JDK 25+ rather than requiring `-Djava.security.manager=allow` globally.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #7430 [Automation] java run fails for ognl:ognl:3.3.5; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: The resolved artifact `ognl:ognl:3.3.5` already brings `org.javassist:javassist:3.29.0-GA`; adding it explicitly is unnecessary unless Gradle resolution later proves otherwise.
JDK 25 removes/blocks SecurityManager installation paths, so tests that assert an installed custom SecurityManager may fail even though the library is usable.

OGNL expression compilation uses Javassist dynamic class generation; native-image runtime tests may need assertions that accept unsupported dynamic generation rather than treating that as missing setup.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: OGNL 3.3.5 is a standalone expression-language library with only transitive javassist runtime support, so no extra Maven dependency or Docker service is needed. Meaningful coverage is reflection-heavy on modern JDKs and should run the test JVM with java.lang opened, as the existing OGNL scaffold already does.
